name: Otter Elite Force — Definitive Ship Plan
description: Complete implementation from engine-rewrite-plan v3.0 through shippable game. LittleJS runtime (not Canvas2D), SolidJS shell, bitECS+templates, 16 missions, full balance. Informed by balance-deep-dive.md, gap-analysis-complete.md, all design docs, and paper playtesting.

userStories.title: Delete Canvas2D runtime, scaffold LittleJS engineInit bootstrap
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the tactical runtime to use LittleJS engineInit() with the 5 standard callbacks (gameInit, gameUpdate, gameUpdatePost, gameRender, gameRenderPost) instead of a hand-rolled requestAnimationFrame loop.
acceptanceCriteria: Delete src/engine/runtime/littlejsRuntime.ts (900-line Canvas2D implementation)
Delete src/engine/runtime/cameraProjection.ts and loopProjection.ts (replaced by LittleJS internals)
Delete src/engine/rendering/ directory (terrainRenderer, spriteRenderer, fogRenderer, combatText, assetLoader — all replaced by LittleJS drawing)
Create src/engine/runtime/tacticalRuntime.ts calling engineInit(gameInit, gameUpdate, gameUpdatePost, gameRender, gameRenderPost, imageSources, rootElement)
imageSources loads all 12 sprite atlas PNGs from public/assets/sprites/
gameInit creates GameWorld, loads templates, bootstraps mission
gameUpdate runs system pipeline + command processor each frame
gameRender draws terrain + entities + fog
gameRenderPost draws minimap + HUD canvas overlays + combat text
Reference: /Users/jbogaty/src/reference-codebases/LittleJS/src/engine.js for engineInit signature
Reference: /Users/jbogaty/src/reference-codebases/wendol-village/main.js for RTS bootstrap pattern
pnpm build passes
pnpm test passes (update/delete tests that reference deleted files)

userStories.title: LittleJS camera and input replacing hand-rolled code
userStories.description: As a developer, I want camera control via LittleJS cameraPos/cameraScale and input via mousePos/keyIsDown instead of custom pointer event handlers.
acceptanceCriteria: Camera position set via setCameraPos()/cameraPos, not custom camera object
Camera zoom set via setCameraScale()/cameraScale, not custom zoom math
Camera clamping uses getCameraSize() for viewport bounds
Mouse position read from mousePos (world space) and mousePosScreen (screen space)
Click detection uses mouseWasPressed(0) for left, mouseWasPressed(2) for right
Key detection uses keyIsDown('KeyA'), keyWasPressed('Digit1') etc
Mouse wheel zoom uses mouseWheel
Touch automatically maps to mouse via LittleJS touchInputEnable
Delete all custom pointer event listeners (onPointerDown/Move/Up/Cancel, onWheel)
screenToWorld() uses LittleJS screenToWorld(), not custom math
Box selection uses mouseIsDown(0) + mousePosScreen delta
Right-drag pan uses mouseIsDown(2) + mouseDelta
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: LittleJS TileLayer for terrain rendering
userStories.description: As a developer, I want terrain rendered via LittleJS TileLayer instead of hand-rolled chunk pre-rendering.
acceptanceCriteria: Create TileLayer from mission terrain grid in gameInit
Use TileLayerData with tileIndex mapping terrain types to sprite sheet positions
Terrain types (grass, water, sand, forest, dirt, stone, mud, mangrove, bridge) each map to a tile index
TileLayer.redraw() called once at mission start and when terrain changes
Terrain tiles loaded as one of the imageSources in engineInit
No custom Canvas2D terrain rendering code remains
Terrain visually shows variety: green grass, blue water, brown dirt, dark forest
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: LittleJS EngineObject subclasses for units, buildings, resources
userStories.description: As a developer, I want game entities to be LittleJS EngineObject instances so they render automatically via the engine's object system.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/engine/objects/GameUnit.ts extending EngineObject — render() draws unit sprite via drawTile with tileInfo from atlas
Create src/engine/objects/GameBuilding.ts extending EngineObject — larger size, faction-colored, building label
Create src/engine/objects/GameResource.ts extending EngineObject — distinct shapes per resource type (tree triangle, fish circle, salvage diamond)
Create src/engine/objects/GameProjectile.ts extending EngineObject — small fast-moving dot with trail
Objects created when entities spawn in GameWorld, destroyed when entities are removed
Object.pos synced from bitECS Position.x/y each frame in gameUpdate
Object.tileInfo set from sprite atlas mapping (unit type → animal atlas → tile info)
renderOrder set to -pos.y for depth sorting
Selection ring drawn in render() when entity is selected
HP bar drawn above entity in render()
Rank emblem drawn for veteran/elite/hero units
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: Aseprite atlas adapter for LittleJS tile() system
userStories.description: As a developer, I want our Aseprite JSON+PNG sprite atlases to work with LittleJS drawTile/tile() so actual otter sprites render.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/engine/rendering/atlasAdapter.ts that parses Aseprite JSON frame data
For each animation tag, compute tile() parameters: pos (pixel offset in sheet), size (frame size)
Create tileInfo objects that drawTile() can use directly
Animation cycling: given a tag name and elapsed time, return the correct frame's tileInfo
Map unit type ID → animal name → atlas → current animation frame tileInfo
All 12 atlases (otter, crocodile, snake, cobra, boar, fox, hedgehog, naked_mole_rat, porcupine, skunk, vulture, weasel) supported
Test: atlas adapter returns correct frame rect for 'Otter Idle' frame 0
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: LittleJS fog of war rendering
userStories.description: As a developer, I want fog of war rendered using LittleJS drawing primitives in gameRenderPost.
acceptanceCriteria: Fog grid (Uint8Array: 0=unexplored, 1=explored, 2=visible) rendered as overlay
In gameRenderPost: iterate visible tiles, draw black rects for unexplored, semi-transparent for explored
Use drawRect() in world space for each fog tile
Entities in unexplored tiles not rendered (skip in EngineObject render)
Fog updates each frame from fogSystem output
Performance: only draw fog tiles within viewport (use isOnScreen)
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: LittleJS minimap in gameRenderPost
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a minimap drawn in screen space during gameRenderPost using LittleJS drawing functions.
acceptanceCriteria: Minimap rendered at bottom-right corner in screen space (200x150px)
Terrain colors from world grid (drawRect per tile in screen space)
Player entities as green dots, enemy as red, resources as yellow
Camera viewport rectangle in white
Fog applied (unexplored areas dark)
Left-click on minimap area snaps camera (screenToWorld conversion)
Right-click on minimap moves selected units to that world position
drawRect and drawCircle with screenSpace=true parameter
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: LittleJS combat text as floating EngineObjects
userStories.description: As a developer, I want damage/heal/resource numbers as short-lived EngineObject instances that float up and fade.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/engine/objects/FloatingText.ts extending EngineObject
Spawned at entity position when HP changes (red '-X' for damage, green '+X' for heal)
Spawned at depot when resources deposited (yellow '+X FISH')
velocity set to upward drift, destroy after 0.9 seconds
render() uses drawText() with world-space positioning
Opacity fades over lifetime via color alpha
No custom Canvas2D overlay code — pure LittleJS objects
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: LittleJS Sound for all audio
userStories.description: As a developer, I want audio using LittleJS Sound class instead of a separate Tone.js audio runtime.
acceptanceCriteria: Delete src/engine/audio/audioRuntime.ts
Create src/engine/audio/sounds.ts using LittleJS Sound class with zzfx arrays

Define all 13 SFX from audio-design.md: click, unit_select, unit_deselect, error, move_order, attack_order, melee_hit, ranged_fire, ranged_hit, unit_death, building_place, building_complete, resource_gather
Define 3 music tracks: menu, combat, briefing (using Sound.playMusic)
Sound.play(pos) for positional audio — sounds near camera are louder
Wire triggers: combatSystem calls melee_hit/ranged_fire/unit_death, commandProcessor calls move_order/attack_order, selection calls unit_select
Use LittleJS soundVolume for master volume, integrated with settings
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: RuntimeHost mounts LittleJS into SolidJS container
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the SolidJS RuntimeHost to mount the LittleJS engine into a DOM container and communicate via GameBridge.
acceptanceCriteria: RuntimeHost.tsx onMount calls engineInit with the container element as rootElement
GameBridge state updated in gameUpdatePost via batch() for SolidJS reactivity
Command queue drained in gameUpdate before systems run
Phase changes (victory/defeat/pause) communicated to SolidJS via bridge signals
Cleanup: engine destroyed in onCleanup
No React patterns (useState, useEffect, useRef) — pure Solid (createSignal, onMount, onCleanup)
HUD overlay panels from SolidJS positioned absolute over the LittleJS canvas
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: ActionBar with context-switching and hotkeys
userStories.description: As a player, I want the action bar to show different commands based on what I have selected (worker/military/building) with keyboard hotkeys.
acceptanceCriteria: Worker selected: Build(B), Gather(G), Move(M), Stop(S) buttons
Military unit selected: Move(M), Attack(A), Stop(S), Patrol(P), Hold(H) buttons
Building selected: Train queue with unit icons, costs, affordability gating
Nothing selected: action bar hidden
Each button dispatches command through bridge.emit
Hotkey labels visible on each button
Buttons disabled when action unavailable (e.g. can't afford unit)
Context hint text below action bar explaining selected action
MilitaryTooltip on hover showing full stats
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: Build placement ghost mode
userStories.description: As a player, I want to click Build, see a translucent building ghost following my cursor, and place it with a click.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking a build button in ActionBar enters placement mode
Ghost building EngineObject follows mousePos with translucent color
Green tint when placement valid, red when invalid (overlap, wrong terrain, insufficient resources)
Left-click places building, deducts resources, creates construction site entity
Right-click or Escape cancels placement mode
Construction site shows progress bar (0-100%) as workers build
Workers auto-rally to nearest construction site
Building completion triggers building_complete sound and alert

Pop cap enforced — can't queue if at population limit

Training complete triggers SFX and alert

Research completion triggers SFX and alert

userStories.title: AlertBanner with 18+ event types and camera snap

userStories.title: CommandTransmission with portraits, typewriter, full dialogue system

userStories.title: Mobile-adaptive layout with phone/tablet/desktop breakpoints
userStories.description: As a mobile player, I want HUD that adapts to my screen size with large touch targets.
acceptanceCriteria: Phone (<640px): stacked layout, large command buttons at bottom, minimap at corner, no tooltip hover
Tablet (640-1024px): side panel layout, medium targets, touch-friendly spacing
Desktop (>1024px): full panel layout with hover tooltips, compact spacing
Form factor detection via matchMedia breakpoints
HUD panels reflow without shrinking — different layouts, not same layout smaller
Touch targets minimum 44x44px on phone and tablet
CommandButtons component for common mobile actions (Move, Attack, Stop, Build)
All interactions work on all form factors — no keyboard-only or hover-only flows
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: Wire JSON templates into entity spawning

spawnBuilding reads HP, armor, buildTime, popCapacity from getBuildingTemplate(id)
Abilities registered on entity from template abilities array
Visual config (sprite, tint, emblem, scale) stored for renderer
Training costs read from template, not hardcoded
Mission-specific overrides from getMissionData(id) applied
Unknown template ID throws error (no fallback)
Test: spawn river_rat, verify HP=40, speed=80, abilities includes 'gather'

userStories.title: Economy with visible gather-carry-deposit cycle
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see workers walk to resources, harvest with animation, carry back to lodge, deposit, and auto-return.
acceptanceCriteria: Worker given gather order walks to resource node
At resource: gather animation plays, timer runs, FloatingText shows gathered amount
Worker picks up resources (carry icon shown above head or tint change)
Worker walks to nearest depot (lodge/command post) at 0.8x speed
At depot: deposit animation, FloatingText shows '+X FISH' at depot position
Worker auto-returns to same resource node
Fish Trap passive income: +3 fish per 10s per trap, visible as periodic FloatingText at trap
Lumber mill bonus: +25% timber gather speed when Lumber Mill exists
Resource node depletion: node shrinks or disappears when exhausted

userStories.title: AI with visible FSM behavior
userStories.description: As a player, I want enemy units to visibly patrol, chase, attack, and flee.
acceptanceCriteria: Idle enemies stand at home position with idle animation
Patrol enemies walk between waypoints with walk animation
Chase: enemy turns toward player unit and moves with run animation
Attack: enemy plays attack animation, combat system handles damage
Flee at <20% HP: enemy turns away and runs, different from normal movement
Group assist: nearby idle enemies join chase when ally detects player
De-aggro: enemy returns to home position when player unit escapes 1.5x vision range
State transitions visible via behavior change, no debug text needed
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: Building construction with visible progress and worker interaction

acceptanceCriteria: Construction site shows building outline at reduced opacity

Workers near construction site play build animation and increase progress
Multiple workers build faster (diminishing returns: 2nd worker +50%, 3rd +25%)
Construction completion: building pops to full opacity, completion SFX, alert
Pop cap increases when population building completes
Defensive buildings (watchtower, gun tower) start attacking when complete
Production buildings can queue units only when construction complete

Elite: gold double-chevron, +20% HP/damage, +5% speed
Hero: star emblem, +30% all stats, special ability slot unlock

HP ratio preserved on promotion (half-health stays half-health)

userStories.title: Save/load roundtrip working in browser
userStories.description: As a player, I want to save mid-mission and resume later.
acceptanceCriteria: Save button in PauseOverlay serializes GameWorld to SQLite
All entity positions, HP, orders, queues, resources, objectives saved
Load deserializes into new GameWorld and resumes at saved tick
Runtime-only objects (nav graph, AI state, Yuka vehicles) reconstructed after load
Campaign progress persisted separately (mission unlocks, stars, best times)
Settings persisted (audio volumes, subtitles, reduce motion)
'Continue' on main menu loads latest campaign save
Test: save at tick 100, load, verify entity positions match

userStories.title: Campaign flow end-to-end with persistence
userStories.description: As a player, I want to play through the campaign with progress saved between sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: New Campaign creates fresh progress (mission 1 available, rest locked)
Mission victory advances campaign (marks complete, unlocks next, records stars/time)

'Continue' loads persisted campaign and goes to next available mission briefing

Best time recorded per mission
Campaign completion screen after mission 16 victory
Difficulty selection persisted (support/tactical/elite)

userStories.title: Balance validation test suite
userStories.description: As a developer, I want automated tests that validate combat and economy balance numbers from balance-deep-dive.md.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: Mudfoot vs Gator 1v1 — Gator wins (120 HP+4 armor vs 80 HP+2 armor)
Test: 2 Mudfoots vs 1 Gator — Mudfoots win
Test: Shellcracker vs Gator — Shellcracker wins by kiting (range 5 vs range 2)
Test: Mortar vs 5 clustered Gators — AoE kills at least 3
Test: First Mudfoot trainable by 2:00 with optimal play
Test: Fish Trap ROI — pays for itself in 250s (75 fish cost / 0.3 fish per second)
Test: Mission 1 completable in 13 minutes with paper-playtest economy timeline
Test: Veteran promotion at 50 XP (5 kills) gives +10% damage
Test: Hardshell Armor research gives +2 armor to all units
All tests deterministic via fixed seed
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: Zero fallbacks, zero debug output, zero legacy
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the production build to be clean with no fallbacks, no debug text, and no legacy framework code.
acceptanceCriteria: grep -r 'from.*react' src/ returns 0 (excluding node_modules and tests)
grep -r 'from.*koota' src/ returns 0
grep -r 'from.*konva' src/ returns 0
No console.log in production code (only console.error for actual errors)
No 'TODO' or 'FIXME' in production code
No shape fallback rendering — missing sprite = error, not colored circle
No debug text overlay on game canvas
Bundle contains zero react/koota/konva chunks
pnpm build passes with zero warnings
pnpm test passes
